revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[dolmen]]s, located in the [[Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a stone coffin, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-shaped stone coffins and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-shaped stone coffin, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[Saga Prefecture]] and [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[Matsuura Railway]] [[Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439538]] Wikidata name: Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s, located in the [[:en:Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[:en:Harayama Dolmen Cluster|Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[:en:Jōmon pottery|Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[:en:Alluvial plain|Alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[:en:Ukujima|Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] and [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[:en:Shito Dolmen Cluster|Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Emukae-Shikamachi Station|Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Jōmon period sites]]
[[Category:Completion tier 5]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439538]] Wikidata name: Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s, located in the [[:en:Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[:en:Harayama Dolmen Cluster|Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[:en:Jōmon pottery|Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[:en:Alluvial plain|Alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[:en:Ukujima|Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] and [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[:en:Shito Dolmen Cluster|Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Emukae-Shikamachi Station|Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s, located in the [[:en:Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[:en:Harayama Dolmen Cluster|Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[:en:Jōmon pottery|Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[:en:Alluvial plain|Alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[:en:Ukujima|Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] and [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[:en:Shito Dolmen Cluster|Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Emukae-Shikamachi Station|Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439538]] Wikidata name: Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s, located in the [[:en:Shikamachi, Nagasaki|Shikamachi]] neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the [[:en:Harayama Dolmen Cluster|Harayama Dolmen Cluster]] on the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]].
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 [[:en:Dolmen|Dolmen]]s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on [[:en:Jōmon pottery|Jōmon pottery]] found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to [[:en:Alluvial plain|Alluvial plain]]s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on [[:en:Ukujima|Ukujima]] island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] and [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], such as the [[:en:Shito Dolmen Cluster|Shito Dolmen Cluster]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Emukae-Shikamachi Station|Emukae-Shikamachi Station]] on the [[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439538]] Wikidata name: Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen}}s, located in the {{ill|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|en|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|WD=Q857910|lt=Shikamachi}} neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the {{ill|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|en|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11409583|lt=Harayama Dolmen Cluster}} on the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula}}.
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen}}s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|WD=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery}} found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}, but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain}}s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on {{ill|Ukujima|en|Ukujima|WD=Q11449014|lt=Ukujima}} island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka}}, such as the {{ill|Shito Dolmen Cluster|en|Shito Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11491118|lt=Shito Dolmen Cluster}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|en|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|WD=Q5374871|lt=Emukae-Shikamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway}} [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The site is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|en|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|WD=Q5374871|lt=Emukae-Shikamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway}} [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster]][[ja:大野台支石墓群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen}}s, located in the {{ill|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|en|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|WD=Q857910|lt=Shikamachi}} neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the {{ill|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|en|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11409583|lt=Harayama Dolmen Cluster}} on the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula}}.
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen}}s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|WD=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery}} found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}, but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain}}s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on {{ill|Ukujima|en|Ukujima|WD=Q11449014|lt=Ukujima}} island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka}}, such as the {{ill|Shito Dolmen Cluster|en|Shito Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11491118|lt=Shito Dolmen Cluster}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|en|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|WD=Q5374871|lt=Emukae-Shikamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway}} [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}}s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on {{ill|Ukujima|en|Ukujima|WD=Q11449014|lt=Ukujima|1=Ukujima}} island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka|1=Itoshima}}, such as the {{ill|Shito Dolmen Cluster|en|Shito Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11491118|lt=Shito Dolmen Cluster|1=Shito Dolmen Cluster}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|en|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|WD=Q5374871|lt=Emukae-Shikamachi Station|1=Emukae-Shikamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | native_name =大野台支石墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Ohnodai shisekibogun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster, Dolmen #15 | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E|region:JP-42|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo, Nagasaki]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Jōmon period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|54.9|N|129|37|8.8|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster'''|大野台支石墓群|Ōnodai shisekibo-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with a late [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] cemetery containing at least 71 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen|1=Dolmen}}s, located in the {{ill|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|en|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|WD=Q857910|lt=Shikamachi|1=Shikamachi, Nagasaki}} neighbourhood of city of [[:en:Sasebo, Nagasaki|Sasebo]], {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2786|title=大野台支石墓群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is the oldest in Japan, along with the {{ill|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|en|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11409583|lt=Harayama Dolmen Cluster|1=Harayama Dolmen Cluster}} on the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula|1=Shimabara Peninsula}}.
==Overview== The Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster is located south of Emukae Bay, on a plateau about 70-80 meters above sea level. It spreads out over an area of about 250 meters east-to-west and 200 meters north-to-south, and is divided into five main sites, A to E, with only Site C and E being included in the National Historic Site designation. In 1963, a local resident discovered a Sarcophagus, and several [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1966 onwards Site C was surveyed in 1966, and eight box-type Sarcophaguss and one jar coffin were found. Site E is located at the highest point, 80 meters above sea level, and 38 {{ill|Dolmen|en|Dolmen|WD=Q101659|lt=Dolmen|1=Dolmen}}s were found there. All of the sites date mainly to the late Jōmon period based on {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|WD=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery|1=Jōmon pottery}} found in or around the dolmens, but at Site E, ritual remains belonging to the late Yayoi period were found, including the handle of a wide bronze spear and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}, but these artifacts are believed to postdate the dolmens. The substructure of the dolmens is mostly a box-type Sarcophagus, which is a characteristic of the dolmens in Nagasaki Prefecture. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
As of 2010, 13 dolmen sites have been confirmed in Nagasaki Prefecture (including this site), and seven of these (including this site), are in the northern part of the prefecture, all of which are adjacent to {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}}s near the coast. It has also been pointed out that there are similarities with the Ukumatsubara Dolmen Site on {{ill|Ukujima|en|Ukujima|WD=Q11449014|lt=Ukujima|1=Ukujima}} island, as well as dolmen sites distributed in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka|1=Itoshima}}, such as the {{ill|Shito Dolmen Cluster|en|Shito Dolmen Cluster|WD=Q11491118|lt=Shito Dolmen Cluster|1=Shito Dolmen Cluster}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavated items and materials are stored and exhibited at the Shikamachi History and Folklore Museum. The site is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|en|Emukae-Shikamachi Station|WD=Q5374871|lt=Emukae-Shikamachi Station|1=Emukae-Shikamachi Station}} on the {{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} [[:en:Nishi-Kyushu Line|Nishi-Kyushu Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.sasebo.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunzai/documents/oonodaisisekibo.pdf Sasebo City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/241 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}
